title: Architecture Proposal
description: Propose the full technical architecture for the universal portfolio + blog template: Next.js App Router, TypeScript, MDX content layer, CSS custom properties theming, Vercel Edge deployment, and a config-driven customisation system.

stack.styling: CSS Modules + CSS Custom Properties (no Tailwind required, but compatible)
stack.content: MDX via next-mdx-remote or @next/mdx, with gray-matter for frontmatter
stack.images: next/image with local and remote support
stack.deployment: Vercel Edge (static export compatible, ISR optional)

directory_structure.app/: Next.js App Router pages and layouts
directory_structure.content/work/: MDX or JSON work item files
directory_structure.public/: Static assets — favicon, OG image fallback, resume PDF

key_architectural_decisions.rationale: Keeps the template dependency-free and avoids a database requirement. Technical users edit TypeScript configs directly. Non-technical users get a well-commented config file that reads like a form.
MDX works for both technical (code blocks, embedded components) and non-technical (plain prose, images) content. Zero CMS dependency means zero recurring cost and no vendor lock-in.
key_architectural_decisions.tradeoff: Content editors need to be comfortable with Markdown. Mitigation: provide a starter content guide.

key_architectural_decisions.rationale: Allows runtime theme switching (light/dark/system) without JavaScript and is compatible with any CSS methodology. Non-technical users change a primaryColor in config and the whole site updates.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Static generation + ISR for blog posts
key_architectural_decisions.rationale: Fastest possible page loads on Vercel Edge. Blog posts are statically generated at build time. ISR allows content updates without a full rebuild for users who want live editing.

key_architectural_decisions.decision: Optional sections via feature flags in nav config
key_architectural_decisions.rationale: Allows a user to deploy a portfolio-only or blog-only variant without deleting code. Sections are conditionally rendered and excluded from sitemap when disabled.
WorkItem schema uses generic fields (title, summary, tags, media) that map equally to a software project, a design case study, a photography series, or a consulting engagement.
key_architectural_decisions.enforcement: TypeScript types + Zod validation at build time

build_time: config/*.ts files are read and validated
CSS tokens from theme config are injected into tokens.css
request_time: CSS custom properties enable theme switching client-side
Contact form POSTs to /api/contact (Resend integration, opt-in)
Vercel Analytics fires on page load (opt-in)

instructions: Present this architecture to the user section by section.
Confirm each key architectural decision — especially MDX vs CMS and config-driven vs UI-driven.
Identify any decisions the user wants to override.
Document confirmed decisions as ADRs in /docs/adr/ before proceeding to threat modeling.
Do not scaffold any files yet.
outputs: List of ADRs to generate in phase 5
guardrails: Do not introduce a database — the template must be zero-infrastructure by default.
Do not introduce a paid CMS — content must be file-based by default.
Do not require a build step beyond next build for any core feature.
All integrations must be opt-in via environment variables.
